description: Detached promise failures may be warnings, process failures, or swallowed runner noise depending on environment.
body: Use when async errors are missing from test output or production logs. Do not silence with a catch that discards context. Ensure promises are returned or awaited, and add rejection assertions. Verify runner output includes the failure before the fix and is clean after. Risk is hidden data loss in background jobs or request handlers. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
